ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  "reports_to" INTEGER /* ID of the supervisor the employee reports to */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the employee */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "address" TEXT /* Street address of the employee */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the employee lives */,
  "state" TEXT /* State or province where the employee lives */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the employee lives */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of the employee's address */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "fax" TEXT /* Fax number of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the employee's emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee's emergency contact */,
  "employee_status" TEXT /* Status of the employee (e.g., active, inactive, terminated) */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was terminated (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_reports_to FOREIGN KEY ("reports_to") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "department_head" INTEGER /* ID of the department head */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_titles" (
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title_name" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job title */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_departments" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the employee belongs to */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee started in the department */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the employee ended in the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_job_titles" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job title the employee has held */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee started in the job title */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the employee ended in the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "supervisors" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "supervisor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supervisor of the employee */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee started reporting to the supervisor */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the employee stopped reporting to the supervisor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "supervisor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being reviewed */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date the review was conducted */,
  "rating" TEXT /* Rating given to the employee */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments about the employee's performance */,
  "goals" TEXT /* Goals set for the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reviews_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password chosen by the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., HR, Manager, Admin) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Permission granted to the user (e.g., view employee data, edit employee data) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "permission")
)

CREATE TABLE "benefits" (
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benefit */,
  "benefit_name" TEXT /* Name of the benefit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the benefit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("benefit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_benefits" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the benefit assigned to the employee */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee started receiving the benefit */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the employee stopped receiving the benefit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "benefit_id")
)
